title: pinus taeda var. palustris
intro: Pinus taeda var. palustris, also known as the longleaf pine, is a variant of the loblolly pine tree that is native to the southeastern United States. The essential oil extracted from the needles of Pinus taeda var. palustris has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and alleviate respiratory issues. The longleaf pine has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including bronchitis, coughs, and skin conditions, due to its medicinal properties. Longleaf pine essential oil can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, salves, and tinctures to harness its medicinal benefits.
